channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Six shot in 'brazen and heartless attack' in the Bronx: NYPD
item.description: THE BRONX, N.Y. (PIX11) -- Six people, including a 12-year-old girl, were shot Monday afternoon in the Bronx, according to the NYPD. The incident happened around 4:50 p.m. at 3544 White Plains Road, police said.
item.dc:creator: Ben Mitchell, NewsNation

item.title: Sen. Blumenthal applauds law that expands Social Security benefits
item.description: WASHINGTON (WTNH) -- Nearly 3 million Americans could see a boost in Social Security payments, thanks to a bipartisan act awaiting President Joe Biden's signature.
item.dc:creator: Eva Zymaris, NewsNation

item.title: 20 cheese factory workers injured after chemical spill at New Mexico factory releases toxic gas: police
item.description: New Mexico authorities say 20 people were injured after a chemical spill at the Southwest Cheese factory produced toxic gas, incapacitating employees.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judge denies sentencing delay for ex-Sen. Bob Menendez but puts his wife's trial off until February
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Former New Jersey Sen. Bob Menendez will be sentenced on corruption charges as scheduled at the end of January, and his wife’s trial will be moved from January to February, a judge said Monday. U.S. District Judge Sidney H.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: FBI issues warning after home of NBA star Luka Doncic targeted in string of burglaries
item.description: The FBI is warning sports leagues about crime organizations targeting professional athletes following a string of burglaries at the homes of prominent NFL and NBA players.
item.dc:creator: Nancy Loo, WGN 9

item.title: Army National Guard soldier from Indiana dies in non-combat incident in Iraq
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A 34-year-old soldier serving with the Indiana National Guard in Iraq has died in a non-combat incident, the Army said Monday. Capt. Eric Richard Hart, 34, of Indianapolis, Indiana, died Saturday in Iraq, the Army National Guard said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Move over, Gen Alpha: 2025 marks the start of the Gen Beta era
item.description: If you just found out about Generation Alpha, brace yourself for a new crop of kids set to debut on Jan. 1.
item.dc:creator: Sakshi Venkatraman, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Cats can get sick with bird flu. Here's how to protect them
item.description: The death of an Oregon house cat and a pet food recall are raising questions about the ongoing outbreak of bird flu and how people can protect their pets.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Southerners are cleaning up from tornadoes that swept the region
item.description: Cleanup is underway after a strong storm system spawned hail, rain, high winds and tornadoes across the southern U.S. over the weekend.
item.dc:creator: Jamie Stengle And Lekan Oyekanmi, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The world population will be 8.09 billion on New Year's Day after a 71 million increase in 2024
item.description: The world population increased by more than 71 million people in 2024 and will be 8.09 billion people on New Year's Day, according to U.S. Census Bureau estimates released Monday. The 0.9% increase in 2024 was a slight slowdown from 2023, when the world population grew by 75 million people.
item.dc:creator: MIKE SCHNEIDER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: NYPD operating in 'heightened threat environment' in advance of New Year's Eve celebration
item.description: The NYPD continues to operate under a "heightened threat environment" as it prepares for New Year's Eve festivities in Times Square.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How to watch WWE's 'Raw' on Netflix: What to know before the Jan. 6 debut
item.description: After 21 years, WWE's "RAW" is leaving cable to join Netflix every Monday night beginning Jan. 6, meaning streaming is the only way to watch the longest-running weekly episodic show.
item.dc:creator: Bill Shannon, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trader Joe's is opening a dozen new locations in 2025: Here's where
item.description: The company’s website currently lists 12 planned locations “opening soon” across seven states and Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, NewsNation

item.title: US sends Ukraine final Biden-approved weapons, financial package
item.description: The Biden administration's final aid to Ukraine before Donald Trump takes office is a $1.25 billion drawdown package.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Appeals court upholds verdict finding Trump liable for sexually assaulting E. Jean Carroll
item.description: A federal appeals court panel on Monday upheld a jury’s verdict finding President-elect Donald Trump liable for sexually assaulting advice columnist E. Jean Carroll and ordering him to pay $5 million.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Returning to the office? Here are some tips to find flexibility and help navigate the changes
item.description: Heading into 2025, thousands of workers face an unsettling reality: After years of working from the comfort of home, they must return to the office full-time for the first time since the coronavirus pandemic or look for new work.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Bussewitz, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What we know about Jimmy Carter funeral plans
item.description: Former President Carter's funeral will be held in Washington early next month as the culmination of roughly a week of remembrances, President Biden announced in a proclamation late Sunday. Carter's state funeral will take place at Washington National Cathedral on Jan. 9, 2025.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ski resort issues warning after guest triggers avalanche
item.description: A popular Colorado ski resort issued a warning after a skier triggered an avalanche in the Christmas Tree Bowl.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, NewsNation

item.title: Linda Lavin, Tony-winning actor who starred in the sitcom ‘Alice,’ dies at 87
item.description: Linda Lavin, a Tony Award-winning stage actor who became a working class icon as a paper-hat wearing waitress on the TV sitcom “Alice,” has died.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Jimmy Carter, the 39th US president, has died at 100
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Jimmy Carter, the peanut farmer who won the presidency in the wake of the Watergate scandal and Vietnam War, endured humbling defeat after one tumultuous term and then redefined life after the White House as a global humanitarian, has died. He was 100 years old.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW and ALEX SANZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: The Latest: State funeral for Jimmy Carter will be Jan. 9
item.description: Former President Jimmy Carter has died at the age of 100. The 39th president of the United States was a Georgia peanut farmer who sought to restore trust in government when he assumed the presidency in 1977 and then built a reputation for tireless work as a humanitarian.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Significant milestones in life and career of Jimmy Carter
item.description: — Oct. 1, 1924: James Earl Carter Jr. is born in Plains, Georgia, son of James Sr. and Lillian Gordy Carter. — June 1946: Carter graduates from the U.S. Naval Academy. — July 1946: Carter marries Rosalynn Smith, in Plains.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Carter to be honored with events in Georgia, Washington
item.description: Former President Carter will be honored with public observances in both Georgia and Washington, D.C., in the coming days, following his death on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Katie Wadington, KRON4

item.title: These glasses let you 'see' conversations in real time
item.description: When I heard about AI glasses that let you "see" what people are saying, I was skeptical. But after trying them on, I was proven wrong.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, WGN 9

item.title: Missing men searching for Sasquatch die in Washington forest
item.description: Two men from Portland searching for Sasquatch and missing since early Christmas morning were found dead in the Gifford Pinchot National Forest, likely from exposure and being ill-prepared.
item.dc:creator: Tim Steele, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Tornadoes in Texas and Mississippi kill 2 and injure 6 as severe weather system moves east
item.description: A strong storm system threatened to whip up tornadoes in parts of the U.S. Southeast on Sunday, a day after severe weather claimed at least two lives as twisters touched down in Texas and Mississippi.
item.dc:creator: Fox 5

item.title: These songs dominated the Billboard charts longest
item.description: Every now and then, a new song releases and dominates radio airwaves, Spotify playlists, and, of course, the esteemed Billboard Hot 100 chart—and in terms of those types of hits, 2024 has been pretty impressive.
item.dc:creator: Angela Underwood, Stacker, Fox 5

item.title: Southern California community among safest small town in America: report
item.description: MoneyGeek, a personal finance site, had researchers analyze last year’s FBI crime data of more than 1,000 small towns and cities where the population is between 30,000 and 100,000.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KTLA 5

item.title: Final crystal triangles installed on Times Square ball ahead of New Year's Eve
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The last of the crystal triangles that make up this year's Times Square New Year's Eve ball were installed on Friday morning, marking the first time in 10 years that all 2,688 were replaced at once.
item.dc:creator: BY MELISSA GOLDIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US homelessness up 18% as affordable housing remains out of reach for many people
item.description: The United States saw an 18.1% increase in homelessness this year, a dramatic rise driven mostly by a lack of affordable housing as well as devastating natural disasters and a surge of migrants in several parts of the country, federal officials said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Michael Casey, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Fox News hosts Sean Hannity, Ainsley Earhardt announce engagement
item.description: "We are overjoyed and so thankful to our families for all of their love and support during this wonderful time in our lives," the couple said.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, ABC 4

item.title: Los Angeles building from famous rock album cover goes up in flames
item.description: Once featured on the cover of a Doors album, the former site of the Morrison Hotel was destroyed by a fire on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, ABC 4

item.title: Former NCAA quarterback Tommy Lazzaro dies in 'tragic' accident at US Air Force base
item.description: Tommy Lazzaro, an Army sergeant and former quarterback for the Central Michigan Chippewas, has died following an accident at Eglin Air Force Base in Florida. He was 27.
item.dc:creator: Matt Jaworowski, Fox 5

item.title: Man indicted in burning death of woman inside New York City subway train: prosecutors
item.description: The indictment comes days after Sebastian Zapeta’s arrest and subsequent police questioning, in which authorities say he claimed not to know what had happened but identified himself in photos and surveillance video showing the fire being lit.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: In states that ban abortion, social safety net programs often fail families
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. (AP) — Taylor Cagnacci moved from California to Tennessee with hopes of starting a new chapter in a state that touts a low cost of living and natural beauty.
item.dc:creator: LAURA UNGAR and KIMBERLEE KRUESI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Climate-friendly electricity sees big battery projects soar again for 2024
item.description: 2024 was another banner year for a source of electricity that is better for people's lungs, better for climate change and may be reaching your home when you turn on the lights or turn up the thermostat — large banks of batteries.
item.dc:creator: ALEXA ST. JOHN and MARY KATHERINE WILDEMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Eye drops recalled nationwide due to fungal contamination
item.description: Check your medicine cabinets: A brand of eye drops sold nationally has been recalled due to the presence of fungus, which may cause vision damage.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Self, ABC 4
